summary: Frontend-only testing of P1 PDF export feature on /dashboard/overview, /dashboard/sales, /dashboard/billing. All 3 PDF buttons produce valid PDFs (correct filename, >1KB, %PDF- magic header). CSV regression intact. Empty-state toast works. Button colors match spec (CSV #3E5C46 green, PDF #8B5A2B brown). Login + role redirect + date browsing regressions still working.
critical_code_review_comments: pdf.js: summary-card label/value columns are hard-coded (label at x=52, value at x=200). Long labels (e.g. 'Offline (Bills)' with trailing '(X bills, avg ₹YYY)' in DSales) may overflow into the value column on narrow content. Minor visual issue, not blocking.

DSales exportPdf includes 'platforms' column whose format can produce very long cell text; jspdf-autotable handles wrapping but may push rows taller. Consider truncating or adding a dedicated platforms section.

DBilling summaryLines treats voided.length as numeric without trailing currency; fine, but value column color (coffee brown #8B5A2B) is applied via doc.setTextColor in pdf.js globally—intentional. No functional issue.
All 3 exportPdf functions duplicate the column definitions inline. If PDF schema changes, 3 files must be updated. Consider extracting shared column-map helpers (DRY).
pdf.js uses doc.text(String(v)) without any cell-length truncation; extremely long strings could break table layout. Acceptable for current data volume.
seed_data_creation: Not needed — today (2026-04-21) already had 2 active bills and analytics data from prior test iterations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: PDF export feature is fully verified via Playwright expect_download. Validation method: suggested_filename prefix/suffix + file size >1KB + first 5 bytes == b'%PDF-'. Button color test should move mouse off element first (mouse.move(10,10) + wait 500ms) otherwise hover state (#704822) is captured instead of base #8B5A2B. Empty-state was reproduced by clicking the chevron-left arrow on /dashboard/billing until date had 0 bills (ThemeDatePicker is a custom button, not a native input, so page.fill() doesn't work on it).

test_results.empty_state_toast_billing: PASS (no download, toast 'No bills to export' shown)
